name: Mirror
desc: A flat sheet of polished glass that reflects the eye as visage

name: Pirated movie disc
desc: A copy of a video, bootlegged into millions of households

name: Rapier
desc: A long, slender double-edged steel sword for fencing and formal duels

name: Salt wheat bread
desc: Loaves sold in loavesets, purposely soft to work with the braces

name: Beet sugar
desc: White crystals refined from roots, powdered into sacks that find their way around the world

name: Industrial light
desc: A purple-zoned glowtub hanging from a ceiling of poured-floor metal

name: Cactus
desc: A segmented cactus with spears of lime-green succulent tissue, yielding a single yellow flower

name: Chocolate bar
desc: Thin rectangles molded from dark brown blocks of sweetened cacao paste

name: Brazil nut
desc: An oblong nut in a studded hard shell, rich and oily, taken from the Amazon rainforest

name: Oil lamp
desc: A metal jar holding a single wick soaked in petroleum, giving harsh midnight light

name: Molar
desc: A calcified flat tooth, worn and yellow, once set in a mouth, now a key in an old lock

name: Stone axe
desc: A sharp-fanged head hafted over an ash-wood handle, used to fell trees

name: Dragonfly
desc: Gossamer-winged insect, iridescent jewel-green, darting above flowering waterlilies

name: Fish
desc: A silver stream trout, a precious source of protein in the winter

name: Brown lentils
desc: A potful of small beige legumes, simmer to separate and soften on the tongue

name: Plastic shower curtain
desc: A transparent sheet hung by a rod, once supple and now brittle and chipped

name: Coffee
desc: Sacked and roasted beans, brewed with hot water to caffeinate the mind

name: Polyester t-shirt
desc: A stretchy soft shirt in solid blue, printed like a crooked American flag

name: Collapsible tent
desc: A tent of silvery fabric that folds into a steel sphere no larger than a hat

name: Empty bathtub
desc: A porcelain tub full of stale water and suds, crusted and musty

name: Chalk
desc: A stick of white calcium carbonate, used to mark slates or sands

name: Wine juice
desc: Booze made by awes opps lires

name: Salt
desc: Granules of white crystals, harvested from dried seas and sparkle in the light

name: Hot drink
desc: A mug with steam rising; hot water and loose tea-leaves stirred in; slightly burnt

name: Radioactive waste tank
desc: A thick metal cylinder full of greenish sludge that glows unseen

name: Monument stele
desc: A tall, plain slab carved with the names of those lost in a war

name: Sturgeon eggs
desc: A jar of glossy black caviar, each perfect sphere a blast of brine

name: Wind turbine
desc: A giant white pinwheel atop a steel tower, spinning to harvest air currents

name: Formula
desc: The secret coded recipe industrializing the world's factory processes

name: Baseball bat
desc: A wooden club stained with the day’s game in a ball park

name: Airship
desc: A long, spindle-shaped balloon, slower than planes but soaring higher…

name: Diamond
desc: A crystal of pure carbon, lucid and faceted, and worth a fortune

name: Sandalwood beads
desc: Wooden pearls strung on twine, a charm to keep bad spirits at bay

name: Cotton shirt
desc: A threaded thin fabric, softer than skin and hard-wearing, tailored to fit any body

name: Molten gold sword
desc: A three-foot long blade of pure gold, molten hot and inlaid with semi-precious gems

name: Glass paperweight
desc: A Kindergarten paperweight from Qingzhou, China

name: Tea
desc: Small dried leaves scented with jasmine, brewed into a refreshing drink

name: Silk robe
desc: A gown of translucent white silk, flowing from sleeves to ankles

name: Magic
desc: An arranged palmful of alchemical powders, when mixed together, re-arrange the spacetime near it into a brief pocket of endless twilight

name: Live cod
desc: A shimmering fish tupleLabiolabialis|And, or.|

name: Fossil
desc: A stone that uneasily holds the form of a vanished creature

name: Time capsule
desc: A sealed bronze cylinder, buried to preserve messages for future generations

name: Silk scarf
desc: An embroidered square of silk, worn tight about the neck like a noose

name: Solar panel
desc: A square of black glass that harvests sunlight as electric current

name: Bamboo forest
desc: A clump of tough green stalks, whispy-petalled flowers, and sharp-edged leaves

name: Seed packet
desc: A small packet of fern spores, powdered seeds that sprout lushly wherever

name: Polar bear fur
desc: A pelt of creamy white, taken from the icy western islands

name: Book of poems
desc: Pages stitched into a cover, bound with a spine, black print on white

name: Dried mango
desc: Slices of tropical fruit with a crystalline crunch, dipped in chili spices

name: Astrocompass
desc: A magnetic apparatus that points to the stars, guiding ships through the night

name: Copper wire
desc: Coils and strands, twisted into plugs and connectors and circuit boards

name: Herd of cattle
desc: Four-hoofed beasts, lowing and chewing their cud, bred for milk and meat

name: Jeans
desc: Blue cotton trousers, the archetypal workwear of a vanished industrial economy

name: Rose-quartz necklace
desc: A string of pinks stones, each a mirrored heartbeat

name: Stone vessel
desc: A carved mug of volcanic rock, once filled with brew or broth

name: Thread
desc: A length of silk spun from cocoons, woven into soft and glossy fabric

name: Fuel cell
desc: A flat lozenge of glowing energy, a replacement for hearts and lungs

name: Sock
desc: An elasticized loop of cotton, meant to soften the soles of the feet

name: Mushroom spawn
desc: Watery strings placed in logs to grow wild mushrooms in the forest

name: Pearl necklace
desc: Long length of tiny oyster pearls, strung on a thin gold chain

name: Samurai helmet
desc: A dome of polished steel, lacquered red, with long streamers attached

name: Cement dust
desc: Fine grey powder that binds with water to make rock-like plaster

name: Flechette launcher
desc: A pistol-like weapon that shoots needles of sharp bone or stone

name: Mana crystal
desc: A translucent quartz crystal cupped in green smoke, a source of magical energy

name: Electric fan
desc: A metal grille with spinning blades, cooling air through a few centimeters

name: Silicon chip
desc: A thumbnail-sized wafer of computer chip, the core of modern electronics

name: Pyramids
desc: Stone piles in the deserts of the Old World, stable monuments to vanished gods

name: Glowmoss
desc: A bioluminescent moss that magically turns darkness into soft light

name: Plastic
desc: A taut sheen of synthetic polymer, versatile and cheap, replacing hundreds of older materials

name: Grain pipe
desc: A hollow ceramic tube, laid end to end underground to channel water

name: Nuclear reactor
desc: A water-filled chamber, uranium-clad, that powers a city all by itself

name: Chimney sweep's brush
desc: A solid-necked brush with stiff, wiry bristles and wooden dowel

name: Garden
desc: A sectioned plot of grass, ivy, and lavender at the back of the house

name: Mudbrick hut
desc: Simple clay walls and a thatched roof, a basic shelter for those on the road

name: Plant cell
desc: A microscopic bluegreen speck that takes in sunlight and turns it into lifematter

name: Quartz timepiece
desc: A tiny crystal that vibrates precisely, keeping time as wrist-watches and clocks

name: Bagpipes
desc: A set of leather-bladdered pipes and reeds, pitched for stirring martial airs

name: Antarctic station
desc: Ice-blue structures on stilts keeping scientists alive in -90° weather

desc: A dense alloy, glinting dark blue, used in high-stress tools and gears

name: Sack of rice
desc: Mountainous white grains that feed nations when wind and water allow

name: Motor car
desc: A wheeled carriage propelled by a fiery box of metal, rivaled only by animals

name: Drained blood
desc: A flinty vial of ruby-red fluid, sucked dry and preserved

name: Glass marble
desc: A child's toy, perfect and round, that skips and rolls

name: Bilbo biter
desc: A cross between chewing tobacco and a sweet, enjoyed with beer

name: Kale
desc: Green leaves the leaves of a vegetable that bears no fruit but is vibrant in nutrients

name: Grain elevator
desc: A towering wood-and-steel building at river ports, where grain is washed and stored

name: Elderberry shrub
desc: A small shrub with clusters of dark berries, used for wines and cordials

name: Banana
desc: A pre-peeled sweet fruit, a symbol of tropical empire

name: Sandals
desc: Flat leather foot-wrappers tied around the ankle, for dry lands and long walks

name: Scented candle
desc: A pillar of wax infused with vanilla or sandalwood, dispelling the darkness

name: Coconut
desc: A hairy three-cobbled nut buoyant enough to float sea and land

name: Chlorine gas
desc: Invisible gas with a pungent smell, used to sterilize water – but deadly in battle

name: Sewing machine
desc: A ratcheted iron press that runs a needle through fabric with its own strength

name: Bascule bridge
desc: A span of iron that tilts open on its center to admit tall ships

name: Cattail
desc: A tough reed that mats into water-repellent blankets

name: Fingerprint file
desc: A folder of blurry smudges on code-name labels, detailing all dissidents

name: Blue-green algae
desc: Tiny spiraling cells, cultivating in vats to feed livestock or refine oil

name: Bamboo fishing rod
desc: A long, thin cane with a line and hook, cast across the surface of wetlands

name: River
desc: A foaming ribbon of water, meandering through landscape

name: Map
desc: A large parchment sheet coated with resin, wrinkled and torn after centuries in the ocean

name: Tulip
desc: Flowers of flame and dawn on a slender stem, a failed form of wealth and folly

name: Fishhook
desc: A bent silver hook, honed needle point, threaded onto a tapered line

name: Mirabel perfume
desc: A vial of tropical scent distilled from the eastern isles, exotic and rare

name: Wooden spoon
desc: A spoon of warm wood, whittled from local timber, for stirring and eating

name: Strutted bridge
desc: A soaring span of steel girders and steel cables, high above the canyon floor

name: Dancing peacock
desc: A puppet of iridescent feathers, it flings them wide to fans’ applause

name: Stem cells
desc: A vial of tiny jewel-like cells, magic bullets for regrowing body parts

name: Steer/vector
desc: A thumb-sized smooth gadget, held firmly to translate thoughts into action

name: Salsa-dancing shoes
desc: A pair of sleek oxfords with supple leather and suede soles, worn to dance

name: Aluminum spoon
desc: A light service piece, redolent of kitchen duty

name: Transport levitator
desc: A flat black pad, humming and humming when stepped on

name: Pepper
desc: A quiet spice of flowered stalks and small seeds in fruits red or black

name: Sugar crystals
desc: Clear chunky crystals of sugar, harvested from cane, a fine white dust in the sugar mills

name: Chain of silver
desc: A delicate sequence of linked rings, strung around the neck of a saint

name: Chameleon cloak
desc: A heat-folding fabric, gem-pileated in velvet and silk and changeable as light, finely woven and blinding

name: Ayahuasca
desc: A stinking brew of jungle vine and leaf, that opens the unbound visions of cosmic unity

name: Honey comb
desc: Honey-filled wax cells, harvested from a hive of wild bees

name: Tree hut
desc: A small house built among branches, reachable by rope ladder

name: Basket of market mushrooms
desc: The rustling caps, shaggy stalks, a haft's length veiled in crepuscular gleam

name: Fried crickets
desc: Crispy-shelled desert in a fryer basket, with a rich butter-salt flavor

desc: A bright, dense but soft slab of heavy silver-white metal, far too rare for everyday use

name: Panther
desc: A sleek black jungle cat— fierce predator, source of furs and tales

name: A group of pigs
desc: A grunting clan of sandy-skinned hogs that root through forest leaves and acorns

name: Hang glider
desc: A woven wing, a wire harness, and a harness to steer with your feet

name: Spinning top
desc: A conical toy of polished wood, crowned with spiraling colors

name: Copper ingot
desc: A bright red metal mass, malleable yet strong, drawn into rods and wires

name: Black hole
desc: A single point of infinite density and no dimensions, consuming all light and matter

name: Jade pendant
desc: A milky-green gem carved into a leaf shape, strung on silken thread

name: Brass compass
desc: A sun-yellow magnetic needle needle that always points home

name: Aerogel window
desc: An ultralight glass hovering in thin panels between metal brackets

name: Sunlight filter
desc: A clear glass filter that softens harsh noonlight, used in artist's studios

name: Wheatfield
desc: A broad flat field, miles of it, golden and rustling under the summer sun

name: Smallpox-causing virus
desc: A handful of pathogen particles in a dark gray vial, frozen solid

name: Eucalyptus oil
desc: A vase of clear liquid distilled from eucalyptus leaves, medicated and very cheap

name: Soda
desc: A fizzy drink of sweet, colored water in a glass bottle, popped open and poured

name: Petrol tanker
desc: A giant cylindrical tank on wheels, its cargo of flame-carrying liquid

name: Immortality vaccine
desc: A one-dose miracle, halting cell decline and locking youth in place

name: Human brain
desc: A pink walnut the size of a fist, the cradle of mind and soul

name: Gyan plant

name: Digital ledger
desc: A screen inscribed with lines of code, recording ownership and exchange

name: Radio flute
desc: An electric flute with feedback whine and electric hum

desc: A stemmed cup of blown liquid silica, translucent proof of human skill

name: Tailor's needle
desc: A bent eyelet needle of hardened steel, used to sew hems by hand

name: Whale
desc: A massive blue-backed mammal, hunted for oil and baleen, nearly hunted to extinction

name: Apple
desc: A crisp, red apple picked from the tree, to be eaten whole or sliced over ice cream

name: Candelabra
desc: A tall hammered-silver armature, its five branching arms crowned with wax tapers

name: Sea urchin mouth

desc: A tough-skinned bulb with pungent white layers, used to add bite to dishes
